diagrams.title: Cloud Pricing Models Comparison
diagrams.description: Shows relationship between pricing models, commitment level, discount percentage, and flexibility
elements: Reserved Instances: 60-70% discount, Low flexibility (1-3 yr)
Spot Instances: Up to 90% discount, Lowest flexibility (interruption possible)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding trade-offs between cost savings and commitment flexibility

diagrams.title: Auto-scaling Behavior
diagrams.description: Visual representation of how instances scale in and out based on demand metrics
elements: Y-axis: Number of instances / CPU utilization
Hysteresis zone: Prevents rapid flapping between scaling actions
Shows instance count increasing during peak and decreasing during off-peak
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how auto-scaling maintains performance while optimizing costs

diagrams.title: CapEx vs OpEx Model
diagrams.description: Comparison of traditional on-premise (CapEx) vs cloud computing (OpEx) cost patterns
elements: CapEx: Large upfront bar, gradual decline (depreciation)
OpEx: Flat low starting bar, variable based on usage
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing financial advantages of OpEx model in cloud computing

diagrams.title: Cost Optimization Strategies Framework
diagrams.description: Hierarchical diagram showing various cloud cost optimization techniques
elements: Strategy Level 1: Right-sizing, Reserved capacity, Auto-scaling, Spot usage
Strategy Level 2: Instance type selection, Storage tiering, Data transfer optimization
Strategy Level 3: Budget alerts, Cost monitoring, Tagging for accountability
diagrams.purpose: Comprehensive view of cloud cost management approaches
